content.problemStatement:
A string is called a Palpal string if it can be divided into contiguous substrings such that:


Each character of the whole string belongs to exactly one substring.
Each of these substrings is a palindrome with length greater than 111.
For example, "abbbaddzcz" is a Palpal string, since it can be divided into "abbba", "dd" and "zcz".

You are given a string sss. You may rearrange the characters of this string in any way you choose. Find out if it is possible to rearrange them in such a way that you obtain a Palpal string.
constraints: sss contains only lowercase English letters
the sum of ∣s∣|s|∣s∣ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single string sss.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if the characters of sss can be rearranged to form a Palpal string or `"NO"` if it is impossible (without quotes).

You may print each character of each string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The string "acbbbadzdz" can be rearranged to "abbbaddzcz", which is the Palpal string mentioned above.
Example case 2: It can be shown that "abcd" cannot be rearranged to form a Palpal string.
Example case 3: The string "xyxyxy" is already a Palpal string, since it can divided into "xyx" and "yxy".
